decks.description: This deck is a legendary-heavy Equipment aggro pile that wants to load up first-strikers with weapons and swing for lethal damage quickly. Balan, Wandering Knight can tap to attach every Equipment you control to itself in one shot, and once it has two or more Equipment attached it gains double strike on top of its innate first strike, letting a single big weapon-stacked hit devastate an opponent. Kwende, Pride of Femeref upgrades that plan further by giving every first striker you control double strike, meaning Danitha Capashen, Syr Alin, or Skyhunter Prowler-style beaters all become double strike threats the moment Kwende is on board. The Circle of Loyalty gets cheaper as your board fills with Knights and spits out 2/2 vigilant Knight tokens whenever you cast a legendary spell (which this deck has plenty of), while also pumping your whole team. Danitha Capashen discounts your Auras and Equipment, smoothing the mana needed to gear up Hero's Blade, Jousting Lance, or Shining Armor, and Hero's Blade auto-attaches to any legendary creature that enters, giving you a free way to arm your legends without spending equip costs.
tips: Attach everything with Balan
Kwende turns first strike into double strike
Cast legends to trigger Circle of Loyalty
Let Hero's Blade auto-equip legends
Use Danitha to cheapen gear

decks.description: This deck plays a grindy artifact-Vehicle midrange game, crewing large Vehicles with a small cluster of crew-efficient creatures and protecting them with combat tricks. Lita, Mechanical Engineer both produces a 5/5 flying Zeppelin token every turn and untaps your other artifact creatures at end of step, letting the same crew members tap to crew again next turn without falling behind on blockers. Hotshot Mechanic and Giant Ox make crewing easier by treating their power (or toughness, for the Ox) as higher than it actually is, letting a single small creature crew Peacewalker Colossus's Crew 4 or Aradara Express's Crew 4 more easily. Aethershield Artificer's combat-phase pump gives a Vehicle +2/+2 and indestructible right as it's turned into a creature, meaning you can crew up, get the buff, and swing in relatively safely, while Caught in the Brights punishes opposing blockers by exiling anything enchanted the moment a Vehicle you control attacks.
tips: Use Ox/Mechanic to ease crewing
Untap crew with Lita each turn
Buff Vehicles pre-combat with Artificer
Attack to exile blockers via Caught in the Brights

decks.description: This is a classic Constellation enchantments deck that wants to chain Auras and enchantment triggers together for repeated value every time an enchantment enters the battlefield. Archon of Sun's Grace creates a 2/2 flying lifelink Pegasus token off every enchantment ETB, Favored of Iroas grants itself double strike on the same trigger, and Captivating Unicorn taps down an opposing blocker or attacker whenever an enchantment resolves, so casting even a single cheap Aura like Forced Worship can simultaneously make a body, buff a creature, and lock down the opponent's board. Sage's Reverie rewards you for stacking multiple Auras on one creature by drawing a card for each attached Aura and pumping that creature accordingly, turning a heavily-enchanted threat into both a card-advantage engine and a real clock, while Spectral Steel can recur itself from the graveyard to fetch back other lost Auras or Equipment.
tips: Chain cheap Auras for triggers
Stack Auras on one creature for Sage's Reverie
Use Captivating Unicorn to tap blockers
Favored of Iroas swings big off any enchantment

decks.description: Similar in spirit to the other Constellation build, this deck leans even harder into flooding the board with Angel tokens and stacking Auras for compounding value. Sigil of the Empty Throne creates a 4/4 flying Angel every time you cast an enchantment spell, which combines directly with Captivating Unicorn's Constellation trigger to both tap down a blocker and generate a huge flier off the same Aura, and Sage's Reverie again turns a multi-Aura creature into a card-draw machine while pumping it proportionally to the Auras attached. Auramancer provides recursion by returning enchantment cards from the graveyard to hand, letting you replay Chains of Custody or Dreadful Apathy to keep answering opposing threats, while Alseid of Life's Bounty can sacrifice itself to protect your key enchanted creature or enchantment from removal at a critical moment.
tips: Cast enchantments for Sigil Angels
Recur Auras with Auramancer
Protect key pieces with Alseid sac
Stack Auras for Sage's Reverie draws

decks.description: This deck is built around 'target creature' triggers that reward you for targeting your own creatures, turning combat tricks and abilities into permanent value. Angelic Cub and Task Force both grow or gain toughness whenever they become the target of a spell or ability, so cheap targeted effects like Infantry Veteran's tap ability, Cavalry Drillmaster's ETB pump, or Sandstone Bridge's land-based buff all double as ways to build a bigger threat over time. Agrus Kos, Eternal Soldier is the centerpiece: whenever an ability targets only him, you can pay {1}{R/W} to copy that ability onto every other creature you control, so activating Infantry Veteran or Taranika's attack trigger on Agrus Kos can blast a buff across your whole team for a small additional cost. Taranika, Akroan Veteran further supports the aggressive swing plan by untapping another attacker and turning it into an indestructible 4/4 when she attacks, letting you push through combat tricks like Valorous Stance and Moment of Triumph without fear of losing your board.
tips: Target Agrus Kos to copy buffs team-wide
Target Angelic Cub/Task Force repeatedly
Use Taranika to swing with indestructible 4/4s
Save Valorous Stance for removal or protection

decks.description: Like its companion build, this deck is centered on rewarding you for targeting your own creatures with spells and abilities, using +1/+1 counters as the payoff. Agrus Kos, Eternal Soldier again lets you copy any targeted ability aimed only at him across your whole board for {1}{R/W}, so targeting him with Infantry Veteran's pump or Iron Bully's ETB counter can spread the effect to every creature you control. Tempered Veteran and Weapon Rack both specifically want +1/+1 counters already in play to build on, with Tempered Veteran cheaply moving counters onto creatures that already have one and Weapon Rack storing three counters to dole out over time, meaning Iron Bully's entering counter or Light of Hope's counter mode feed directly into more counter-based growth. Angelic Cub and Task Force again benefit from being targeted repeatedly, growing tougher or eventually gaining flying, making this a grindy counters-and-targeting value deck rather than a pure beatdown list.
tips: Copy target abilities via Agrus Kos
Seed counters then grow with Tempered Veteran
Use Weapon Rack to spread counters
Target Angelic Cub for eventual flying

decks.description: This is a low-to-the-ground white Spirits tribal deck that turns creature deaths into more flying bodies and grinds out card advantage through recursion. Doomed Traveler and Mausoleum Guard both leave behind 1/1 flying Spirit tokens when they die, and Blessed Defiance explicitly sets up a sacrifice-for-value play by giving a creature +2/+0 and lifelink while promising a Spirit token if that creature dies in combat that turn, so you can trade away a buffed attacker and still come out ahead on board. Gallows Warden buffs every other Spirit's toughness, making your swarm of tokens harder to remove in combat, while Angel of Flight Alabaster returns a Spirit card from your graveyard to hand every upkeep, letting you replay Doomed Traveler or Apothecary Geist for repeated ETB and death triggers. Chains of Custody offers a rare piece of interaction, exiling a problematic opposing permanent as long as the Aura stays attached.
tips: Trade Blessed Defiance creature for a token
Recur Spirits with Angel of Flight Alabaster
Buff toughness via Gallows Warden
Chump attack knowing deaths make tokens

decks.description: This Spirits build shifts toward a convoke-powered board wipe and swarm strategy, using its small creatures to help cast expensive sweepers and go-wide spells cheaply. Hour of Reckoning and Triplicate Spirits both have convoke, so tapping down Doomed Traveler, Mausoleum Guard, or the tokens they leave behind lets you cast a full board wipe or three fresh 1/1 flying Spirits well ahead of curve, and since Hour of Reckoning only destroys nontoken creatures, your own token Spirits survive the wipe while the opponent's board is wiped clean. Devouring Light similarly uses convoke to cheaply exile an attacker or blocker, and Martyr's Soul rewards you for going first with no tapped lands by entering with two extra +1/+1 counters. Death triggers from Mausoleum Guard and Doomed Traveler keep feeding your convoke pool with fresh flying bodies, creating a loop where dying creatures replace themselves and immediately help power out your next big spell.
tips: Convoke off token Spirits
Hour of Reckoning spares your tokens
Death triggers refuel convoke fodder
Cast Martyr's Soul on an empty board

decks.description: This deck is a value-oriented white creature deck built around ETB recursion and incremental counter growth rather than any single flashy combo. Pillardrop Rescuer and Miraculous Recovery both return creatures from the graveyard (to hand or directly to the battlefield with a +1/+1 counter, respectively), while Pilgrim of the Ages can itself return from the graveyard for a late-game mana sink, giving the deck real staying power in grindy games. Goldnight Commander turns every creature entering into a temporary team-wide pump, so playing Pillardrop Rescuer or Miraculous Recovery's reanimated body at the right moment can push through extra combat damage across the board. Angelic Cub and Anointer of Valor both build up +1/+1 counters over time — Angelic Cub whenever it's targeted, Anointer of Valor by paying {3} on attack — giving the deck a slow-building threat alongside its recursive value creatures and removal like Divine Arrow and Chains of Custody.
tips: Recur creatures with Pillardrop/Miraculous Recovery
Time ETBs with Goldnight Commander swings
Grow Angelic Cub via targeting
Pay into Anointer of Valor on attacks

decks.description: This is an Angel tribal deck that leans on Lyra Dawnbringer and Starnheim Aspirant to make its top-end fliers both cheaper and much stronger. Starnheim Aspirant discounts Angel spells by {2}, letting you deploy Lyra Dawnbringer, Emancipation Angel, or Dawning Angel ahead of schedule, and once Lyra Dawnbringer is out, every other Angel you control picks up +1/+1 and lifelink, turning even small Angels into sustainable lifegain threats. Defy Death doubles as both recursion and Angel-specific upside, returning any creature from the graveyard to the battlefield but granting two extra +1/+1 counters if the returned creature is an Angel, making it a strong way to rebuy Lyra Dawnbringer or another Angel after it dies. Stalwart Valkyrie's alternate cost lets you cheat it into play early by exiling a creature card from your graveyard instead of paying full mana, and Pilgrim of the Ages plus Emancipation Angel's bounce-on-ETB give you extra utility and land smoothing along the way.
tips: Discount Angels via Starnheim Aspirant
Lyra buffs all other Angels
Rebuy Angels with Defy Death
Cheat out Valkyrie via graveyard exile

decks.description: This deck is a lifegain payoff pile built around triggering big life swings and turning them into card advantage, board presence, and pump. Inspiring Cleric and Dawning Angel each pad your life total by 4 the moment they land, and Valkyrie Harbinger watches for any turn you gain 4 or more life to spit out a free 4/4 flying, vigilance Angel token at end step, so those Clerics and Angels are basically pre-loaded tokens waiting to happen. Dawn of Hope converts every lifegain trigger into an optional card draw for {2}, letting a single Light of Hope or Chains of Custody activation refill your hand while Serene Steward turns the same lifegain events into +1/+1 counters for {W}. Chains of Custody and Divine Arrow give you interaction against attackers and blockers, while Emancipation Angel and Pilgrim of the Ages provide flexible value and land smoothing. The overall plan is slow and grindy: stack incidental life gain, snowball it into extra bodies and cards, then close with buffed fliers.
tips: Chain lifegain triggers each turn
Hit 4+ life for free Angels
Use Dawn of Hope to draw off lifegain
Save Chains of Custody for threats
Play Clerics before attacking

decks.description: This deck doubles down on the lifegain-matters theme with an even tighter payoff engine centered on Righteous Valkyrie, who not only gains you life equal to the toughness of every Angel or Cleric that enters but also gives your whole team +2/+2 once you're 7 life above your starting total. Inspiring Cleric, Daybreak Chaplain's lifelink, and Pillardrop Rescuer's grave-recursion all feed that life total, while Attended Healer turns your first lifegain trigger each turn into a free 1/1 Cat token and can grant another Cleric lifelink to keep the loop going. Pilgrim of the Ages and Pillardrop Rescuer add resilience by recurring themselves or other creatures from the graveyard, and removal like Chains of Custody, Faith's Fetters, and Divine Arrow keeps the board clear while you build toward the Valkyrie's team-wide buff. The gameplan is to stack Angels and Clerics on curve, hit the 7-life threshold quickly, and swing with a suddenly much bigger board.
tips: Play Angels/Clerics for Valkyrie triggers
Race to 7 life over start
Attended Healer nets free Cats
Use Faith's Fetters/Chains for removal+life
Recur creatures with Pillardrop Rescuer

decks.description: This is a defensive white tempo-control deck that leans on tapping down opposing creatures and locking pieces down with Arrest, Divine Verdict, and Valorous Stance while grinding value from creatures leaving play. Magnanimous Magistrate is the centerpiece: it enters with five reprieve counters and lets you return nontoken creatures that die back to the battlefield by spending counters equal to their mana value, so cheap creatures like Gideon's Lawkeeper or Midnight Guard dying can come right back for free reanimation. Ninth Bridge Patrol grows every time another creature you control leaves the battlefield, meaning trades, sacrifices, or Magistrate's own graveyard shuffling all pump it into a real threat, while Midnight Guard untaps whenever any creature enters, letting it ambush block or reactivate Gideon's Lawkeeper's tap ability. Righteousness is a blowout combat trick that can wreck an attacker, and Kami of Ancient Law offers enchantment hate. Overall the deck wants to control the board with taps and removal, trade efficiently to feed Ninth Bridge Patrol and Magnanimous Magistrate, then win with a slow grinding advantage.
tips: Trade small creatures to fuel Magistrate
Feed Ninth Bridge Patrol with deaths
Chain Midnight Guard untaps
Save Righteousness as a trick
Lock threats with Arrest

decks.description: This deck is built around board control via tapping and pacifism effects, backed by go-wide finishers. Law-Rune Enforcer and the general tap theme keep opposing creatures from attacking while Arrest and Unquestioned Authority neutralize individual threats (with Unquestioned Authority also replacing itself with a card). Magnanimous Magistrate again rewards creature death by letting you pay reprieve counters equal to a fallen creature's mana value to bring it back, synergizing with Ninth Bridge Patrol's +1/+1 counters whenever a creature leaves the battlefield, and Midnight Guard's untap trigger keeps your taps and blocks coming. The deck's finishers are Decree of Justice, which can either be cycled early for a wave of 1/1 Soldiers or hard-cast late for a pile of 4/4 flying Angels, and Phalanx Tactics, which pumps the whole team for a surprise alpha strike. Radiant's Judgment offers flexible removal or cycling when the board doesn't call for it. The game plan is to stall with taps and Arrests, grind extra value from creatures dying, then close with a wide, buffed attack.
tips: Tap down attackers each turn
Cycle Decree of Justice early, hardcast late
Recycle deaths into Magistrate reanimation
Grow Ninth Bridge Patrol off deaths
Alpha strike with Phalanx Tactics

decks.description: This is a control-oriented white deck anchored by Gideon, Champion of Justice, whose +1 loyalty grows based on how many creatures the opponent controls, letting him become a huge indestructible attacker with his 0 ability if the opponent has built a board, and his ultimate can wipe every other permanent in play. The deck backs him up with tap effects like Gideon's Lawkeeper, hard removal in Angelic Edict and Arrest, and the combat trick Righteousness to blow out an attacker. As in the other Law decks, Magnanimous Magistrate turns creature deaths into free reanimation by spending reprieve counters equal to the fallen creature's mana value, and Ninth Bridge Patrol grows every time a creature you control leaves the battlefield, rewarding trades and sacrifices. Unquestioned Authority both draws a card and makes a creature nearly unkillable in combat. The overall plan is to stall the board with removal and tapping, protect and grow Gideon, and eventually swing for lethal with his indestructible creature mode or wipe the board with his ultimate.
tips: Grow Gideon off opponent's board
Swing safely with Gideon's 0 ability
Reanimate via Magnanimous Magistrate
Feed Ninth Bridge Patrol with deaths
Save Righteousness for a blowout

decks.description: This deck plays a defensive, disruptive game centered on locking down and punishing the opponent's creatures rather than racing. Michiko Konda, Truth Seeker forces the opponent to sacrifice a permanent every time one of their sources damages you, discouraging aggression, while Edifice of Authority slowly builds brick counters to first stop a creature from attacking and eventually lock it down entirely. Arrest and Bring to Trial provide hard removal for big threats, and Murder Investigation turns a creature you control dying into a swarm of 1/1 Soldier tokens equal to its power, meshing with Magnanimous Magistrate's ability to spend reprieve counters equal to a fallen nontoken creature's mana value to bring it straight back from the graveyard. Ninth Bridge Patrol again grows whenever any of your creatures leaves the battlefield, so trading creatures away (especially ones enchanted with Murder Investigation) generates tokens, counters, and potential reanimation all at once. Isamaru, Hound of Konda gives an efficient early clock while the rest of the deck sets up its lock pieces.
tips: Enchant a creature with Murder Investigation before it trades
Let creatures die to fuel Magistrate/Patrol
Build brick counters on Edifice of Authority
Punish attackers with Michiko Konda
Save Bring to Trial for big threats

decks.description: This is a Cat tribal aggro deck that curves out with efficient beaters and lords, then pushes damage through with pump and protection. Regal Caracal is the key payoff: it makes two 1/1 lifelink Cat tokens on entry and gives all your other Cats +1/+1 and lifelink, while King of the Pride stacks another +2/+1 onto every Cat you control, turning even the tokens into serious threats. Basri's Acolyte adds +1/+1 counters to up to two other creatures on entry, and Ingenious Leonin can pump an attacking Cat and grant it first strike for {3}{W}, letting a buffed Cat both hit hard and survive combat. Make a Stand is the deck's key combat trick, turning a full alpha strike into an indestructible, +1/+0 swing that blanks removal and most blocks. Weight of Conscience offers Cat-specific enchantment removal by tapping two same-typed creatures, which is easy to enable in an all-Cat deck. The plan is simple: flood the board with lifelinking Cats buffed by lords, then finish with a protected alpha strike.
tips: Play Regal Caracal for tokens+lord
Stack King of the Pride for team pump
Use Make a Stand to push lethal
Pump attackers with Ingenious Leonin
Tap two Cats to pop enchantments

decks.description: This Cats deck blends tribal synergy with a landfall subtheme, rewarding you for playing lands every turn as much as for casting Cats. Steppe Lynx gets +2/+2 whenever a land enters, Prowling Felidar and Felidar Retreat both trigger off landfall to add +1/+1 counters or make 2/2 Cat Beast tokens (or buff the whole team with vigilance), and Trove Warden's landfall exiles a cheap permanent from your graveyard only to return everything exiled under it when Trove Warden dies, effectively banking a recursion payoff. King of the Pride still pumps every Cat you control by +2/+1, and Make a Stand protects your board and pushes extra damage through as a finisher. The deck wants to hit every land drop on curve to maximize Steppe Lynx, Prowling Felidar, and Felidar Retreat triggers simultaneously, snowballing board size and stats turn after turn, while Impeccable Timing and Weight of Conscience handle problematic blockers or enchantments.
tips: Hit every land drop for landfall
Stack Steppe Lynx/Prowling Felidar triggers
Let Trove Warden die to reclaim exiles
Buff team with King of the Pride
Protect attackers with Make a Stand

decks.description: This deck is a lifegain-Cats hybrid built around Ajani's Pridemate and its namesake planeswalker Ajani, Strength of the Pride, both of which convert any lifegain into permanent growth. Ajani's Pridemate gets a +1/+1 counter every time you gain life, so Basri's Acolyte's counters, Savannah Sage's 2 life, and the lifelink damage from Trained Caracal and Basri's Acolyte all directly feed it, while Ajani himself gains you life equal to your total creature and planeswalker count with his +1, snowballing your life total and Pridemate's size together; his -2 even creates a second copy of Ajani's Pridemate to double up on that scaling, and his ultimate can wipe the opponent's board once you've gained enough life. King of the Pride buffs every Cat by +2/+1 on top of this, and Ingenious Leonin can push an attacking Cat further with a +1/+1 counter and first strike. Make a Stand protects the team during a big swing. The overall plan is to gain as much incidental life as possible to grow one or two Pridemates into enormous threats while the rest of the Cats provide a supporting go-wide clock.
tips: Gain life to grow Pridemates
Play multiple Ajani's Pridemate copies
Ultimate Ajani after enough lifegain
Buff team with King of the Pride
Protect with Make a Stand

decks.description: This is a straightforward Cat tribal aggro deck that curves efficient lords and evasive threats into a wide, lifelinked attack. Leonin Warleader is the standout payoff, creating two tapped-and-attacking 1/1 lifelink Cat tokens every time it attacks, which immediately benefits from King of the Pride's +2/+1 buff to all Cats and turns a single attack into three extra bodies of damage and life gain. Basri's Acolyte adds +1/+1 counters to up to two other creatures on entry, Skyhunter Prowler provides an evasive vigilance flier, and Pouncing Lynx gets first strike on your turn to win combat. Ingenious Leonin can pump and grant first strike to an attacking Cat for {3}{W}, and Make a Stand turns a full team attack into an indestructible alpha strike that survives combat tricks and removal. Weight of Conscience and Impeccable Timing round out the deck's answers to blockers and enchantments. The plan is simple: build a wide Cat board, attack repeatedly with Leonin Warleader to keep generating extra lifelink tokens, and close with a protected swing.
tips: Attack with Leonin Warleader for tokens
Stack King of the Pride buffs
Use Make a Stand for lethal
First strike via Pouncing Lynx/Ingenious Leonin
Clear blockers with Impeccable Timing

decks.description: This deck is built around ETB (enters-the-battlefield) triggers that get reused via blink/flicker effects, generating repeated value from a small set of key creatures. Distinguished Conjurer and Inspiring Overseer reward you for creatures entering with life gain and card draw, while Preston, the Vanisher turns every non-cast entrance (blinks, tokens) into a free 0/1 Illusion copy that can eventually be sacrificed five-at-a-time to exile any nonland permanent. The core engine is Wispweaver Angel and effects like Acrobatic Maneuver, Flicker of Fate, and Settle Beyond Reality re-triggering ETBs on creatures like Rambunctious Mutt (destroy artifact/enchantment), Brightmare (tap + lifegain), or Gallant Cavalry (make a Knight token) for repeated removal, tokens, and lifegain. Faith's Fetters provides a lifegain-attached removal option, and Thraben Inspector adds early card advantage. The plan is to grind out incremental advantage each turn by replaying your best ETB creatures rather than relying on raw stats.
tips: Blink Wispweaver Angel repeatedly
Save Preston's Illusions for removal
Re-trigger Rambunctious Mutt on threats
Use Distinguished Conjurer for life buffer
Sequence ETBs to maximize value

decks.description: Like other Blink builds, this deck leans on ETB triggers reused through flicker effects, but adds a combat-buff angle via Emiel the Blessed and Imperial Aerosaur. Emiel the Blessed can repeatedly exile and return your own creatures for {3}, re-triggering ETBs like Rambunctious Mutt's artifact/enchantment destruction or Valorous Steed's Knight token creation, while also handing out +1/+1 counters whenever a creature enters. Imperial Aerosaur and Daybreak Charger provide temporary combat tricks (flying and +2/+0) that combine well with blink effects like Justiciar's Portal, which re-triggers an ETB and grants first strike. Distinguished Conjurer and Inspiring Overseer keep the life total and card flow steady as you loop creatures in and out of play. The overall plan is grindy value generation with occasional combat pushes rather than a fast clock.
tips: Use Emiel to loop ETBs each turn
Time Imperial Aerosaur for lethal swings
Blink Rambunctious Mutt for repeat removal
Stack counters via Emiel's trigger
Keep Faith's Fetters for problem threats

decks.description: This build doubles down on flicker synergies with Preston, the Vanisher and Restoration Angel providing repeatable ways to reuse ETB abilities. Preston creates a 0/1 Illusion copy whenever a nontoken creature enters without being cast (i.e., via blink), letting effects like Flicker of Fate or Settle Beyond Reality generate free token copies of your best creatures, which can be sacrificed five at a time to exile any nonland permanent. Restoration Angel adds a flash blink option that also reuses Cavalry Drillmaster's combat buff or Rambunctious Mutt's destruction effect. Valor in Akros rewards you for the flurry of creatures entering by giving your whole board +1/+1 until end of turn whenever any creature enters, turning a blink turn into a surprise team pump for combat. Distinguished Conjurer and Inspiring Overseer again supply the steady lifegain and card draw that keep this grindy plan resilient.
tips: Flicker creatures to trigger Valor in Akros
Stack Illusion copies for Preston's sac ability
Flash in Restoration Angel end of turn
Reuse Rambunctious Mutt on key permanents
Use Valor in Akros to close out combat

decks.description: This version of the Blink deck adds Panharmonicon, which doubles ETB triggers from creatures and artifacts, dramatically amplifying the value of cards like Wispweaver Angel (double blink chances), Seller of Songbirds (two Bird tokens), Pierce Strider (opponent loses 6 life instead of 3), and Rambunctious Mutt (destroy two artifacts/enchantments off one entrance). Distinguished Conjurer and Inspiring Overseer also double their lifegain and draw with Panharmonicon in play, making it the deck's clear payoff card around which everything else is built. Flicker of Fate and Settle Beyond Reality let you re-trigger these doubled ETBs on demand, and Faith's Fetters offers a lifegain-attached lockdown for problem permanents. The strategy is to land Panharmonicon as early as possible and then chain blinks for exponential value rather than board presence alone.
tips: Prioritize landing Panharmonicon early
Double Pierce Strider for 6 life loss
Blink Wispweaver Angel for chain blinks
Double up Rambunctious Mutt destruction
Protect Panharmonicon once resolved

decks.description: This deck is a tribal-synergy pile built around Changelings (creatures that count as every creature type) and copy effects that can mimic whatever creature type matters most. Bloodline Pretender and Littjara Kinseekers reward having creatures that share a type — since every Changeling in the deck (Universal Automaton, Amoeboid Changeling, Mistwalker, Littjara Kinseekers itself) shares all types with each other, Bloodline Pretender grows with every Changeling that enters, and Littjara Kinseekers gets a counter and scry as long as three creatures share a type. Amoeboid Changeling can strip or grant creature types to enable Heirloom Blade's tribal-tutor-on-death effect or to make an opponent's creature lose relevant types. Pirated Copy and Gigantoplasm copy any creature on the battlefield (including your opponent's), letting you steal a strong body while gaining a card-draw or scalable X/X mode, and Mirror Image can copy your best in-deck creature for redundancy. Lookout's Dispersal and Crashing Tide provide tempo-based interaction that gets cheaper or gains flash when you have a Pirate or Merfolk in play, which the Changelings can supply.
tips: Play Changelings early for tribal count
Copy opponent's best creature
Use Amoeboid Changeling to enable Heirloom Blade
Trigger Littjara Kinseekers with 3 shared types
Grow Bloodline Pretender with Changelings

decks.description: This deck's entire game plan revolves around snow permanents and paying with {S} mana to unlock powerful abilities, tapping down opponents while building toward the Marit Lage's Slumber payoff. Isu the Abominable lets you play from the top of your library and grows with a +1/+1 counter whenever another snow permanent enters (paid for with G, W, or U), while Marit Lage's Slumber scries whenever a snow permanent enters and eventually sacrifices itself to create a 20/20 indestructible flying Marit Lage token once you control ten or more snow permanents. Berg Strider, Icebind Pillar, Winter's Rest, and Crippling Chill all use {S} to tap down and lock opposing creatures, working alongside Phyrexian Ironfoot's repeatable snow-fueled untap and Avalanche Caller's ability to animate snow lands into 4/4 hasty hexproof threats. The deck plays a tempo/control game, snowballing incremental taps and card advantage until it can either grind out the opponent or slam down the Marit Lage token.
tips: Count snow permanents for Marit Lage
Use {S} abilities whenever possible
Lock down blockers with Winter's Rest
Animate lands with Avalanche Caller
Grow Isu off every snow permanent

decks.description: This deck is a classic tempo-control Faeries build centered on flash creatures that punish opponents for tapping out and reward casting spells on their turn. Spellstutter Sprite counters a spell based on the number of Faeries you control, which is boosted by having Pestermite, Sentinels of Glen Elendra, and other cheap Faeries on board. Brineborn Cutthroat and Wavebreak Hippocamp both explicitly reward casting spells during an opponent's turn — Brineborn grows with a +1/+1 counter each time, while Wavebreak draws you a card off your first such spell each turn — turning your countermagic and flash creatures into incidental value engines. Glen Elendra Pranksters lets you bounce your own creatures (like ETB value pieces or Vendilion Clique) in response to threats whenever you cast an instant-speed spell on the opponent's turn, protecting your board while re-using enter-the-battlefield effects like Vendilion Clique's hand disruption. Instruments of War and Fleeting Distraction round out the disruption suite, making this a deck that wants to hold up mana and interact reactively rather than commit to the board early.
tips: Hold mana for opponent's turn
Count Faeries before Spellstutter Sprite
Bounce creatures with Glen Elendra Pranksters
Chain flash spells for Wavebreak Hippocamp
Rebuy Vendilion Clique with bounce effects

decks.description: A close cousin to the other Faeries deck, this build again plays a flash-heavy tempo game but leans more on tap-down effects and a big payoff bounce spell. Stinging Lionfish rewards casting your first spell on an opponent's turn by letting you tap or untap a nonland permanent, functioning much like Pestermite's ETB tap/untap ability to either tap down a blocker or untap your own mana. Stolen by the Fae is the deck's standout card, bouncing a creature based on its mana value while simultaneously creating that many 1/1 flying Faerie tokens, single-handedly generating both tempo and a board of evasive blockers/attackers. Glen Elendra Pranksters again offers protective bounce whenever you cast a spell on the opponent's turn, and So Tiny scales up from -2/-0 to -6/-0 once the opponent's graveyard reaches seven cards, synergizing naturally with Synchronized Eviction and other cheap answers filling their yard over the game. Faerie Seer's scry and Undersea Invader's flash-in body smooth draws while keeping mana open for reactive plays.
tips: Hold up mana each turn
Bounce big threats with Stolen by the Fae
Tap blockers with Stinging Lionfish
Save So Tiny for late game
Protect board with Glen Elendra Pranksters

decks.description: This deck is a blue tempo-value pile built around bounce effects and looting (draw-then-discard) that combo with self-mill and graveyard recursion. Barrin, Tolarian Archmage bounces a creature or planeswalker on entry and then draws you a card at end step if any permanent was returned to hand that turn — this synergizes directly with Tolarian Sentinel and Wizard Mentor, both of which can bounce your own permanents (including Barrin itself) to re-trigger his ETB and the card draw. Merfolk Pupil's loot ability doubles as graveyard fuel since it can later be exiled from the graveyard for another loot, while Academy Journeymage gets cheaper with a Wizard in play (easily enabled by Kasmina's token or Merfolk Pupil) and bounces an opponent's creature for tempo. Overwhelmed Apprentice mills opponents while scrying for you, and Kasmina, Enigmatic Mentor taxes opposing removal on your board while her -2 makes tokens and loots. The overall plan is to out-value opponents with repeated bounces, loots, and card selection rather than big threats.
tips: Rebounce Barrin for extra draws
Use Wizard Mentor to reset ETBs
Play Wizard first for cost reduction
Loot aggressively with Merfolk Pupil
Protect Kasmina with cheap Wizard tokens

decks.description: This deck continues the bounce-and-loot blue package but shifts toward a longer grind fueled by Rhystic Study and Multiple Choice as card-advantage payoffs. Rhystic Study punishes opponents who don't pay the tax, letting you draw extra cards nearly every turn, while Multiple Choice scales from a simple scry-and-draw at X=1 up to bouncing a creature and even creating a 4/4 Elemental token if X is high enough, rewarding you for ramping mana. Academy Journeymage again gets cheaper off a Wizard (easily supplied by Kasmina's -2 token or Teferi's Protege) and bounces an opponent's creature, while Campus Guide finds a land to keep your mana curve smooth for casting bigger spells like Multiple Choice. Merfolk Pupil, Elite Instructor, and Teferi's Protege all loot to filter draws and dig toward answers or threats, and Bury in Books discounts itself against attackers for cheap removal. This build plays a slower, more resilient control game than its counterpart, aiming to bury the opponent in incremental card advantage.
tips: Land Rhystic Study early
Ramp toward big Multiple Choice
Loot away excess lands
Use Wizard tokens to cheapen Journeymage
Bounce attackers with Bury in Books

decks.description: This mono-blue-leaning deck (splashable via Thriving Isle) is built entirely around manipulating the top of the library and then cashing in on that manipulation. A huge number of creatures and spells scry when they enter or resolve — Biblioplex Kraken, Faerie Seer, Sage's Row Savant, Octoprophet, Preordain, Interpret the Signs, and No Escape all scry, and Moonfolk Puzzlemaker scries every time it's tapped (including from attacking). Kenessos, Priest of Thassa is the central payoff: it adds one to every scry you make, turning small scries into big ones and helping dig extra deep, while its activated ability lets you cheat a Kraken, Leviathan, Octopus, or Serpent (like Biblioplex Kraken or Cryptic Serpent) directly onto the battlefield off the top of the library. Interpret the Signs rewards the deck's card selection directly: after scrying 3 (which becomes 4 with Kenessos) you reveal the new top card and draw cards equal to its mana value, so all that shuffling to the top pays off in a burst of cards. The deck otherwise plays a patient, grindy game, using Perilous Voyage and No Escape for tempo/interaction while setting up your draws for a smoother, more consistent late game.
tips: Play Kenessos early to boost every scry
Stack scries before Interpret the Signs
Use Kenessos's ability to cheat in sea beasts
Sequence scries before drawing
Hold Perilous Voyage for tempo swings

decks.description: Like its sibling, this deck is a mono-blue scry pile anchored by Kenessos, Priest of Thassa, which adds one to every scry and can put a Kraken, Leviathan, Octopus, or Serpent onto the battlefield straight off the top of the library. Faerie Seer, Augury Owl, Octoprophet, Biblioplex Kraken, and Preordain all scry on entry or cast, filtering away clunky draws and stacking the deck toward action, while Moonfolk Puzzlemaker scries whenever it's tapped, including on attack. This build leans a bit more into card advantage and inevitability than Scrying 1: Djinn of Wishes lets you repeatedly reveal and cast the top card of your library for free (or exile it if you decline), which synergizes well with all the scrying that's setting up good hits, and Condescend both protects your plan with a soft counter and scries 2 to keep digging. Anchor to the Aether and Explorer's Scope round out the deck with tempo and land-finding utility, but the core gameplan remains grinding out card quality advantages until you can bury the opponent in more relevant threats and answers.
tips: Scry before activating Djinn of Wishes
Kenessos amplifies every scry effect
Use Kenessos to cheat in sea creatures
Save Condescend to protect key turns
Use Explorer's Scope to hit land drops

decks.description: This is a 'draw two' payoff deck: nearly every creature cares specifically about drawing your second card in a turn. Faerie Vandal and Jace, Arcane Strategist both put a +1/+1 counter on a creature when you draw your second card, Mystic Skyfish grants itself flying off that same trigger, and Steelgaze Griffin gets +2/+0 from it, while Eyekite and Tome Anima key off having drawn two-plus cards that turn to get bigger or become unblockable. Merfolk Pupil and Teferi's Protege are cheap loot effects that exist specifically to trigger these 'second card' abilities on demand, letting you turn on Faerie Vandal or Jace's counter whenever you want rather than waiting on a natural second draw. Soul Read, Serum Visions, and Drag Under are extra card-draw spells that also help hit that second-draw threshold, and Memorial to Genius provides a late-game land that cracks for two cards. The overall plan is to flood the board with evasive, growing threats that all get better the more you loot and draw, turning simple card filtering into snowballing stats and evasion.
tips: Loot with Merfolk Pupil to trigger draw-two
Use Teferi's Protege to fish for second draws
Sequence draws to hit two per turn
Play Jace early for steady counters
Save Drag Under to reset blockers

decks.description: Built around the same 'second card drawn' trigger as its siblings, this deck's headline card is Alandra, Sky Dreamer, who creates a 2/2 flying Drake token every time you draw your second card in a turn, and then pumps herself and all Drakes by X (your hand size) whenever you draw a fifth card in a turn. Faerie Vandal, Mystic Skyfish, and Steelgaze Griffin all key off that same second-draw trigger for counters, flying, or a power boost, while Merfolk Pupil and Teferi's Protege are repeatable loot effects that let you manually trigger these abilities rather than waiting for a natural second draw. Eternity Snare and Hedron Archive add extra card draw (and a way to lock down a blocker) to help push toward the second- and even fifth-card thresholds, with the deck's late-game payoff being multiple pumped-up flying Drakes swinging in after a big draw turn. The game plan is to loot early and often to generate tokens off Alandra, then chain draws in a single turn to combine her token generation with the fifth-card pump for a lethal swarm.
tips: Loot to trigger Alandra's token making
Stack multiple draws in one turn
Chain to a fifth draw for a team pump
Use Eternity Snare to stop key blockers
Crack Hedron Archive late for extra cards

decks.description: This version of the draw-two archetype swaps in cycling cards as its main enabler instead of dedicated loot creatures. Alandra, Sky Dreamer still creates a flying Drake token off your second draw each turn and pumps the team on a fifth draw, while Faerie Vandal, Mystic Skyfish, and Steelgaze Griffin get counters, flying, or extra power from that same second-draw trigger. Neutralize, Startling Development, Lay Claim, and Desert of the Mindful all have cycling costs, letting you discard them for a card whenever they're not needed, which is an easy, repeatable way to manufacture that all-important second draw of the turn. Merfolk Pupil and Teferi's Protege still provide direct loot effects to double up on draws when cycling alone isn't enough, and Hieroglyphic Illumination's cheap cycling cost or its two-card draw mode gives more flexibility. The deck plays like a value/tempo pile that turns 'excess' cards in hand into free triggers for its creatures, aiming to consistently hit two draws a turn to snowball Drakes and pumped attackers.
tips: Cycle extra cards to hit second draw
Sequence cycling before your draw step
Use Startling Development for surprise attacks
Save Lay Claim for a stolen threat
Chain cycles to fuel Alandra's tokens

decks.description: This build of the draw-two theme trades some payoff creatures for a wider suite of repeatable draw engines and combat synergy. Faerie Formation and Spectral Sailor both let you pay mana to draw a card at instant speed, giving you on-demand access to a second draw to trigger Faerie Vandal's counter, Mystic Skyfish's flying, or Steelgaze Griffin's pump. Mantle of Tides is a direct payoff for the mechanic, automatically attaching itself to a creature you control whenever you draw your second card each turn, effectively granting a free equip and stat boost turn after turn. Military Intelligence rewards the deck's evasive, wide boards by drawing a card whenever you attack with two or more creatures, meshing with Tome Anima's unblockable clause and the deck's several flyers to keep cards flowing from combat as well as from spells. Merfolk Pupil and Teferi's Protege remain the deck's flexible loot pieces, and Memorial to Genius offers a late land-based card draw burst, all combining into a deck that wants to attack with a crowd of evasive creatures while constantly refilling its hand.
tips: Attack wide to trigger Military Intelligence
Use Spectral Sailor at instant speed
Let Mantle of Tides auto-equip for free
Loot with Teferi's Protege for second draw
Push Tome Anima through as unblockable

decks.description: This is an artifact/Clue synergy deck that generates card advantage through investigating and then leverages the resulting Clue tokens as an artifact count for its payoffs. Drownyard Explorers, Floodhound, Press for Answers, Jace's Scrutiny, Hold for Questioning, and Magnifying Glass all create Clue tokens, flooding the board with artifacts. Filigree Attendant's power equals your artifact count and Shambling Suit's power equals your artifacts and enchantments, so both creatures grow directly off of every Clue you crack out or leave on the battlefield. Gearseeker Serpent gets cheaper with Affinity for artifacts, letting all those Clues discount it into an efficient evasive threat, while Vedalken Engineer taps for two mana usable only on artifacts to help cast and activate everything. The most explosive interaction is Dutiful Replicator copying an existing Clue token (or other token) for extra value, and Mechanized Production can turn a mass of identical artifact copies into an alternate win condition if you can get eight matching artifacts. Overall the deck plays a grindy artifact-value game, converting Clues into both card draw and raw stats.
tips: Crack Clues to grow Filigree Attendant
Hold Clues to boost Shambling Suit
Copy Clues with Dutiful Replicator
Use Vedalken Engineer to fuel artifacts
Watch for a Mechanized Production win

decks.description: This deck combines the Clue-generation package with a 'draw a card, drain/grow' payoff suite that punishes players for every card they draw. Psychosis Crawler scales its power and toughness with your hand size and pings each opponent for 1 life whenever you draw a card, while Oneirophage grows a +1/+1 counter on every draw, both of which are triggered constantly by Drownyard Explorers, Floodhound, Press for Answers, Jace's Scrutiny, and Hold for Questioning cracking or creating Clues. Tolarian Kraken adds a tap/untap effect on every card drawn, which can be used offensively to tap down blockers or defensively to untap your own creatures, again triggered by the deck's abundant draw sources. Teferi's Puzzle Box forces both players to wheel their hands every draw step, which massively multiplies your draw triggers for Psychosis Crawler and Oneirophage (at the cost of also refilling your opponent), and Aeronaut Tinkerer gets flying for free as long as you control any artifact, which the Clue tokens easily provide. This is a value-drain deck that wants to draw as many cards as possible to slowly grind the opponent's life total down while its creatures balloon in size.
tips: Crack Clues to trigger draw payoffs
Teferi's Puzzle Box multiplies draw triggers
Grow Oneirophage every draw
Use Tolarian Kraken to tap blockers
Keep an artifact up for Aeronaut Tinkerer flying

decks.description: This build pushes the Clue-artifact theme toward an artifacts-matter aggro/tempo shell with tap effects and a big beater. Lumengrid Sentinel and Weldfast Wingsmith both trigger whenever an artifact you control enters — Lumengrid Sentinel lets you tap down a permanent (great for clearing blockers or locking down a threat) and Weldfast Wingsmith grants itself flying — and since the deck creates Clue tokens constantly via Drownyard Explorers, Floodhound, Press for Answers, Jace's Scrutiny, and Hold for Questioning, both triggers fire repeatedly off cheap artifact creation. Skilled Animator turns any artifact, including a Clue token, into a 5/5 creature for as long as it stays on the battlefield, giving the deck a way to convert card-draw fodder into a genuine threat. Shimmer Dragon rewards having four or more artifacts with hexproof, protecting your flying finisher, and can also tap two artifacts to draw a card, meshing with all the Clues lying around. Dutiful Replicator can copy a Clue or other token for extra value. The deck plays a proactive game of flooding the board with artifacts to tap down opposing creatures, turn Clues into an unexpected 5/5, and protect its dragon with hexproof.
tips: Animate a Clue into a 5/5
Tap blockers with Lumengrid Sentinel
Protect Shimmer Dragon with four artifacts
Copy Clues with Dutiful Replicator
Crack extra Clues to draw off Shimmer Dragon

decks.description: This build layers a tap-based control plan and hand-size payoffs on top of the Clue-generation core. Tamiyo, the Moon Sage taps down and locks a permanent with her +1, and her -2 draws a card for every tapped creature an opponent controls, which becomes especially strong if you've been locking things down turn after turn. Erdwal Illuminator doubles up on card advantage by giving you an extra investigate the first time you investigate each turn, meaning cards like Drownyard Explorers, Press for Answers, Jace's Scrutiny, and Hold for Questioning (which already create Clues on their own) net you two Clues instead of one. Syr Elenora, the Discerning has power equal to your hand size and draws a card when she enters, growing naturally as this Clue-heavy deck fills your hand, while her tax on targeted removal helps her survive. Tamiyo's Journal generates a free Clue every upkeep and can be sacrificed along with two other Clues to tutor for any card in your deck, giving the deck a late-game answer or finisher. The overall plan is to lock down the board with taps, generate a glut of Clue-fueled card advantage, and let Syr Elenora and Tamiyo's card draw close out the game.
tips: Double Clues with Erdwal Illuminator
Lock permanents with Tamiyo's +1
Draw big off Tamiyo's -2 vs tapped boards
Grow Syr Elenora with a full hand
Tutor via Tamiyo's Journal with three Clues

decks.description: This deck is a mono-blue tribal aggro-tempo build that wants to flood the board with unblockable and evasive Merfolk while using tap effects to keep the way clear. Merrow Reejerey pumps the whole team and lets you tap down blockers or untap lands every time you cast another Merfolk spell, which combos directly with River Sneak (which itself grows every time a Merfolk enters) and Storm Sculptor, whose enters-the-battlefield bounce can reset a tapped Watertrap Weaver to tap another blocker down. Watertrap Weaver and Storm Sculptor both punish the opponent's board while Seafloor Oracle and Sage of the Falls turn combat damage and creatures entering into card draw, keeping your hand full of gas. Aquatic Incursion adds two hexproof Merfolk tokens that are hard to interact with and also grants a repeatable unblockable ability, letting you push through damage with One With the Wind or Crashing Tide backing you up as tempo plays. Overall this is a low-curve, evasive tribal deck that wins by chipping in with unblockable threats and bouncing back problematic blockers rather than grinding out card advantage.
tips: Chain Merfolk spells for Reejerey taps
Lead with Watertrap Weaver on blockers
Protect unblockable threats first
Use Aquatic Incursion tokens as hexproof beaters
Draw off combat damage triggers

decks.description: This build leans harder into card advantage and protection than raw aggression, anchored by Svyelun of Sea and Sky, which becomes indestructible once you have two other Merfolk and grants the whole tribe ward 1, making your board very hard to remove or bounce profitably. Merrow Reejerey's tap-or-untap trigger synergizes with River Sneak's growth and with Watertrap Weaver to repeatedly lock down blockers, while Svyelun's attack trigger and Windrider Patrol's combat-damage scry keep your draws relevant. Saltwater Stalwart turns any connection into a card for either player you choose (usually yourself), and Synchronized Eviction becomes a cheap tempo-negative bounce-to-library effect once you have Merfolk sharing a type, which is trivial here. Merfolk Pupil doubles as a looting engine and later a graveyard-recursion card filter. The deck plays a grindy, resilient Merfolk shell that out-values opponents through ward-protected threats and repeated card selection rather than pure speed.
tips: Build to 2+ Merfolk for Svyelun's indestructible/ward
Attack with Svyelun to draw cards
Use Synchronized Eviction cheaply with tribal synergy
Loot with Merfolk Pupil early and late
Tap down blockers with Watertrap Weaver

decks.description: This version of the Merfolk deck is built around a tap/untap engine that generates repeated value and unblockable damage rather than sheer speed. Wake Thrasher grows every time any permanent you control untaps, which combos beautifully with Stonybrook Angler's repeatable tap-or-untap ability and Merrow Reejerey's cast trigger, letting you pump Wake Thrasher multiple times a turn just by untapping your own stuff. Fallowsage flips this on its head by drawing a card whenever it becomes tapped, so Merrow Reejerey or Stonybrook Angler tapping it down nets you cards while also feeding Wake Thrasher's untap-matters trigger over subsequent turns. Harbinger of the Tides offers a flash tempo play that bounces a tapped attacker, and Watertrap Weaver plus Synchronized Eviction round out the disruption suite. This is a tempo-value hybrid Merfolk deck that wants to tap and untap its own permanents for incidental value while chipping in with evasive threats like Triton Shorestalker and Windrider Patrol.
tips: Tap Fallowsage repeatedly for draws
Untap permanents to pump Wake Thrasher
Use Stonybrook Angler on your own creatures
Flash in Harbinger of the Tides on tapped attackers
Scry with Windrider Patrol to filter draws

decks.description: This is the most straightforwardly aggressive Merfolk build, leaning on evasive lords and unblockable creatures to close games quickly. Merfolk Sovereign and Merrow Reejerey both pump the tribe and add ways to force damage through — Sovereign taps down blockers on a repeatable activated ability while Reejerey taps or untaps a permanent every time you cast a Merfolk spell, working directly with River Sneak's per-creature-entering growth trigger to make it a fast-growing unblockable clock. Aquatic Incursion adds two hexproof token bodies plus a repeatable unblockable-granting ability for {3}{U}, letting even non-evasive creatures like Library Larcenist or Saltwater Stalwart sneak through for their card-draw triggers on combat damage. With Watertrap Weaver and Soul Read/Crashing Tide providing just enough interaction, the deck's plan is simple: flood the board with unblockable threats, pump them with lords, and let combat damage triggers refill your hand as you race to the finish.
tips: Curve out Merfolk lords early
Use Sovereign/Incursion to push damage through
Grow River Sneak with more Merfolk entering
Cash in combat damage for cards
Hold Crashing Tide for tempo swings

decks.description: This artifacts deck is built around Thopter token generation feeding both an aerial swarm and a value engine, all pointed toward Tezzeret, Artifice Master as a long-game payoff. Whirler Rogue, Aviation Pioneer, and Launch Mishap each pump out 1/1 flying Thopters, which count toward Tezzeret's 0 ability drawing two cards instead of one once you have three-plus artifacts, and also fuel Whirler Rogue's own tap-two-artifacts unblockable ability to push key threats through. Skilled Animator turns any artifact, including a Thopter token, into a temporary 5/5 beater, giving you a surprise finisher out of cards that would otherwise just be small fliers. Gearsmith Prodigy and Gearsmith Guardian both scale off your artifact and blue-creature count, becoming efficient beaters once the board fills up. The overall gameplan is to go wide with cheap flying artifact tokens while digging with Merfolk Pupil and Artificer's Epiphany, eventually landing Tezzeret to bury the opponent in card advantage or artifact tokens.
tips: Flood board with Thopter tokens
Feed Tezzeret's card draw with artifact count
Animate a Thopter into a 5/5 with Skilled Animator
Use Whirler Rogue to push key threats unblocked
Loot with Merfolk Pupil for selection

decks.description: This build blends the Thopter-artifact shell with a small equipment/removal package built around Renowned Weaponsmith tutoring up Heart-Piercer Bow or Vial of Dragonfire. Heart-Piercer Bow pings a blocker for 1 whenever the equipped creature attacks, while Vial of Dragonfire is a one-shot 2-damage artifact sacrifice, giving the deck actual answers to small creatures that the rest of the format lacks. Hangarback Walker is the centerpiece grindy threat: it grows with counters via its own activated ability and then explodes into that many Thopter tokens when it dies, meaning removal aimed at it backfires into a swarm of fliers. Aeronaut Tinkerer and Gearsmith Prodigy both get better simply from having any artifact around, and Juggernaut provides a hard-to-block ground beater that must attack each turn. The deck plays as an artifact aggro-midrange list, using Whirler Rogue and Aviation Pioneer tokens as chip damage and blockers while Hangarback Walker and the Weaponsmith package provide reach and removal.
tips: Grow Hangarback Walker before it trades
Let Hangarback die into Thopter swarm
Tutor Bow or Vial with Weaponsmith
Equip Heart-Piercer Bow to ping blockers
Force through Juggernaut on the ground

decks.description: This artifact deck is built to go wide with Thopters and then pump the whole team with Steel Overseer, which puts a +1/+1 counter on every artifact creature you control each turn it's untapped. That combos cleanly with the constant stream of tokens from Whirler Rogue, Aviation Pioneer, Thopter Spy Network's upkeep trigger, and Whirlermaker's repeatable activated ability, turning a horde of 1/1 fliers into a genuinely threatening army over just a couple of turns. Clockwork Hydra offers a separate counters-matters payoff, entering with four counters and converting them one at a time into 1 damage to any target whenever it attacks or blocks, giving you reach and removal in one card, while also being able to regrow counters with its own tap ability. Thopter Spy Network doubles as a card advantage engine, drawing a card whenever your artifact fliers connect, which rewards the wide boards this deck naturally builds. This is a token-swarm-plus-counters deck that wants to flood the sky with Thopters, buff them all at once with Steel Overseer, and grind out cards off Thopter Spy Network.
tips: Untap Steel Overseer before attacking
Stack Thopters before activating Overseer
Ping with Clockwork Hydra in combat
Draw cards via Thopter Spy Network swarm
Use Whirlermaker for late-game token stream

decks.description: This is the most value/toolbox-oriented of the artifact decks, centering on Self-Assembler tutoring for Assembly-Worker creatures like Mishra's Factory (a land that becomes a 2/2 Assembly-Worker) and the actual Assembly-Worker card, both of which get pumped by Assembly-Worker's own and Mishra's Factory's tap abilities. Dutiful Replicator lets you pay {1} to copy any token you control, meaning it can double a Thopter from Whirler Rogue or Aviation Pioneer for extra board presence, and Cogwork Assembler can copy any artifact on the battlefield (including a land-creature like an activated Mishra's Factory) for a burst of temporary value with haste. Solemn Simulacrum smooths your mana while also drawing a card when it eventually dies, and Gearsmith Prodigy again scales off simply having artifacts in play. This deck plays a slower, toolbox-y game, using Self-Assembler and Cogwork Assembler to fetch and copy exactly the artifact or land-creature it needs while Whirler Rogue and Aviation Pioneer provide steady incidental flying tokens.
tips: Tutor Assembly-Worker with Self-Assembler
Copy tokens with Dutiful Replicator
Animate Mishra's Factory as extra threat
Copy key artifacts with Cogwork Assembler
Ramp and replace with Solemn Simulacrum

decks.description: This mono-black Rats tribal deck grinds opponents down through discard, recursion, and a scaling team-wide anthem effect. Ogre Slumlord is the engine piece: whenever another nontoken creature dies (yours or the opponent's), you get a free 1/1 Rat token, and it also gives all your Rats deathtouch, turning even your smallest bodies into real threats and trade fodder. Ashcoat of the Shadow Swarm pushes this further, pumping every other Rat by X (the number of Rats you control) whenever it attacks or blocks, and its end step mill-and-return ability can bring back up to two Rat creatures from your graveyard to refill the board. Crypt Rats doubles as a board wipe and reach, dealing X damage to every creature and player off black mana, which combos with Ogre Slumlord since any of your Rats that die from it can generate more tokens. Burglar Rat, Chittering Rats, and Nezumi Bone-Reader add a steady disruption package of hand and card-quality attacks, while Ulcerate and Eviscerate provide hard removal. The deck's plan is to keep making Rats, keep growing them with Ashcoat and Ogre Slumlord, and strip the opponent's hand and options along the way.
tips: Trade Rats to trigger Ogre Slumlord
Grow team with Ashcoat before combat
Recur Rats via Ashcoat's mill ability
Use Crypt Rats as a one-sided-ish sweeper
Strip hands early with Burglar/Chittering Rats

decks.description: This is a highly built-around-a-number drain deck whose entire gameplan revolves around pushing opponents to exactly 13 life so Triskaidekaphobia can simply end the game, or using -13/-13 effects to blank any creature outright. Tree of Perdition is the most direct enabler, letting you exchange an opponent's life total with its toughness (which can be set precisely), setting up a kill with Triskaidekaphobia's upkeep trigger that makes any player at exactly 13 life lose the game. Tragic Slip and Ruthless Disposal both key off the number thirteen too: Tragic Slip's morbid clause turns a -1/-1 into a blowout -13/-13 if a creature died that turn, and Ruthless Disposal always hits two creatures for -13/-13 as a sacrifice-and-discard removal spell. Along the way, a suite of small life-drain triggers — Sling-Gang Lieutenant's sacrifice ability, Gnawing Zombie, Skirsdag Supplicant, Soulcage Fiend's death trigger, Dread Rider, and Exsanguinate — chip opponents down (or up, if you overshoot) toward that magic number while gaining you matching life. This deck plays a grindy, numbers-obsessed drain/control game where every point of life loss is calculated toward landing the opponent on exactly 13.
tips: Track opponent's life total closely
Land them on exactly 13 for Triskaidekaphobia
Use Tree of Perdition to set life precisely
Trigger morbid before Tragic Slip
Sac fodder into Sling-Gang/Gnawing Zombie drains

decks.description: This mono-black deck leans on sacrifice and life-loss synergies to grind opponents down while cheating out powerful Demons. Demon's Disciple forces a mass sacrifice the moment it enters, thinning boards and setting up Kothophed, Soul Hoarder to draw cards whenever an opponent's permanent dies. Disciple of Perdition rewards careful life-total management: if you can land on exactly 13 life, its death trigger lets you both draw a card and mill/punish the opponent's graveyard instead of choosing one. Removal like Demon's Grasp and Diabolic Edict clears blockers or forces bad sacrifices, while Inner Demon turns any creature into a flying Demon and wipes the rest of the board with a -2/-2 pulse, synergizing with the deck's Demon-tribal payoffs like Takenuma Bleeder. Infernal Idol provides late-game card advantage by cracking for two cards when the mana isn't needed elsewhere.
tips: Land life total on exactly 13 for Disciple of Perdition
Use Demon's Disciple to strip blockers
Inner Demon turns any body into a board wipe
Crack Infernal Idol late for cards
Protect Kothophed to keep drawing off dying permanents

decks.description: A mono-black Demon-tribal sacrifice deck that pairs disruptive sacrifice effects with heavy card draw at the cost of life. Demon's Disciple again forces both players to sacrifice, feeding Dreadhound's mill-and-drain ability whenever creatures die or are milled. Seizan, Perverter of Truth is the standout engine here, forcing every player to draw two cards and lose two life on their upkeep — a double-edged card advantage machine that this deck's life-gain and drain effects can help offset better than opponents. Doomed Dissenter turns removal or sacrifice fodder into a 2/2 Zombie, while Demonic Gifts can protect a key creature from a sacrifice effect or combat trick by returning it after it dies. Diabolic Edict and Demon's Grasp round out the removal suite, letting this deck control the board while its card-advantage engines pull ahead over a longer game.
tips: Deploy Seizan for a card-draw war
Use Demon's Disciple to snipe key blockers
Demonic Gifts saves creatures from sac effects
Doomed Dissenter turns removal into value
Race the life loss with drain effects

decks.description: This mono-black self-mill deck fills its own graveyard as fast as possible to power up graveyard-scaling payoffs. Stitcher's Supplier, Wailing Ghoul, and Gorging Vulture all mill on entering (or dying), stocking the yard for Revenant, whose power and toughness equal the number of creature cards in your graveyard, and Ossuary Rats, which blasts a creature for X damage based on that same count. Necrotic Wound and Moodmark Painter both scale off graveyard creature count too, turning a big graveyard into either a removal spell or an evasive attacker. Oversold Cemetery and Pit Keeper let you recur creatures from the yard once you hit four or more creature cards there, while Suspicious Shambler can be exiled from the graveyard for two 2/2 Zombies, giving you a way to convert a full graveyard into board presence even after your recursion targets run dry.
tips: Mill early to fuel Revenant and Ossuary Rats
Hit 4+ creatures in yard for Oversold Cemetery/Pit Keeper
Necrotic Wound scales with graveyard size
Exile Suspicious Shambler for tokens late
Sequence mill creatures before big X spells

decks.description: Another mono-black self-mill deck, this build doubles down on graveyard-count payoffs as direct threats and win conditions rather than just recursion. Stitcher's Supplier, Wailing Ghoul, and Gorging Vulture again fill the yard, while Liliana's Elite grows permanently with every creature card in the graveyard and Graveblade Marauder converts combat damage into direct life loss equal to that same count, turning a stocked graveyard into a fast clock. Ossuary Rats and Necrotic Wound provide graveyard-scaled removal, and Ghoul's Feast can push through lethal damage by pumping an attacker's power by the graveyard count. Shadowborn Demon offers unconditional removal on a flying body but demands upkeep sacrifices unless you keep at least six creatures milled, tying its own maintenance cost back into the deck's mill plan.
tips: Mill aggressively to grow Liliana's Elite
Graveblade Marauder punishes big graveyards
Keep 6+ creatures milled to feed Shadowborn Demon
Ghoul's Feast for surprise lethal
Use Necrotic Wound as scaling removal

decks.description: This mono-black Morbid deck wants a creature to die every turn to unlock its best effects, turning combat and sacrifice into removal and value. Tragic Slip goes from a minor -1/-1 to a devastating -13/-13 once something has died, and Reaper from the Abyss destroys a non-Demon creature at end step under the same condition, so cheap sacrifice fodder like Doomed Dissenter-style trades or Village Rites (which also draws two cards off a sacrifice) sets up blowouts. Bone Picker becomes a nearly free flying deathtouch threat once Morbid is active, while Wakedancer and Morkrut Banshee reward the same death trigger with a token or -4/-4 removal. Spawning Pit converts excess sacrifices into 2/2 Spawn tokens over time, and Mortuary Mire recurs a key creature by putting it on top of your library, letting the deck rebuild its death-trigger engine turn after turn.
tips: Trigger a death before big Morbid spells
Tragic Slip is a blowout post-Morbid
Village Rites for cheap card draw
Cast Bone Picker after something dies
Mortuary Mire recurs key creatures

decks.description: This mono-black deck builds around cheap sacrifice fodder to consistently enable Morbid triggers and cheat out huge Demon tokens. Priest of the Blood Rite immediately creates a 5/5 flying Demon, while Skirsdag High Priest can tap two creatures (after something has died that turn) to make another 5/5 flyer, giving this deck an unusually high density of big evasive threats for a value shell. Reassembling Skeleton is a perfect sacrifice engine, dying repeatedly to feed Morbid conditions and Village Rites/Vampiric Rites for card draw or life gain, then returning from the graveyard for {1}{B}. Tragic Slip again scales into a -13/-13 removal spell once Morbid is active, and Wakedancer and Morkrut Banshee reuse the same death triggers for tokens and removal, making this a tight loop of sacrifice-enable-payoff.
tips: Reassembling Skeleton fuels repeated Morbid
Time Tragic Slip after a death
Priest/Skirsdag High Priest for big flyers
Vampiric Rites turns sac fodder into cards
Sequence deaths before Wakedancer/Morkrut Banshee

decks.description: This mono-black deck punishes opponents for drawing cards and taking damage, building a slow drain-and-ping engine around Ob Nixilis, the Hate-Twisted and Syr Konrad, the Grim. Ob Nixilis pings opponents for 1 whenever they draw, and his -2 ability trades a creature for card disadvantage-neutral removal since the opponent draws two cards but loses the creature; Syr Konrad deals 1 damage to opponents every time a creature dies or is milled anywhere, including via Ob Nixilis's own removal and Syr Konrad's own mill ability. Termination Facilitator turns any damage-based effect into removal by placing bounty counters, and Talon of Pain stockpiles charge counters off incidental damage from Creeping Bloodsucker or Wicked Guardian to later blast a finishing shot. Feast on the Fallen quietly grows a creature every upkeep as long as the opponent keeps losing life, which this deck's constant pinging makes nearly automatic.
tips: Syr Konrad punishes every creature death
Bounty counters + damage = removal
Feed Talon of Pain with incidental damage
Ob Nixilis -2 baits card draw for removal
Feast on the Fallen grows almost every turn

decks.description: This mono-black deck weaponizes small amounts of damage into hard removal, letting cheap pingers and enchantments finish off anything that's been touched. Plague Spitter deals 1 damage to every creature and player on your upkeep and again when it dies, which combos directly with Mire Blight (destroy enchanted creature when it's dealt damage) and Termination Facilitator's bounty counters (destroy any bountied creature that takes damage), turning incidental pings into a one-sided board wipe. Lurking Deadeye flashes in to finish off anything already damaged that turn, and Consign to the Pit is a clean kill spell that also burns the controller for 2. Nested Ghoul actually benefits from being pinged, converting damage taken into 2/2 Zombie tokens, giving this deck some resilience against its own damage-based synergies.
tips: Plague Spitter pings enable Mire Blight kills
Bounty counters turn pings into removal
Nested Ghoul profits from being damaged
Lurking Deadeye cleans up damaged creatures
Consign to the Pit for reach plus removal

decks.description: This mono-black deck combines repeatable damage effects with counter-based threats to grind out removal and reach. Cruel Sadist doubles as a mana sink, growing with +1/+1 counters that can later be stripped off to deal direct damage to a creature, while Hooded Assassin and Mire Blight both punish anything that's taken damage that turn by destroying it outright. Festering Evil pings everything for 1 every upkeep and can later sacrifice for a 3-damage board-wide finisher, feeding both Mire Blight and Hooded Assassin's kill conditions. Dread Slaver is the deck's standout synergy piece: any creature it damages and kills in combat returns to the battlefield under your control as a Zombie, letting you steal opposing threats outright. Talon of Pain and Feast on the Fallen provide additional long-game value off the deck's steady stream of direct damage and drain from Creeping Bloodsucker.
tips: Festering Evil sac ability finishes damaged creatures
Dread Slaver steals what it kills
Hooded Assassin/Mire Blight punish any damage
Cruel Sadist doubles as removal mana sink
Stack pings before big attacks

decks.description: This mono-black deck pairs steady incidental drain with damage-triggered removal to slowly bury the opponent. Dread Presence turns every Swamp drop into either a card (at the cost of 1 life) or a 2-damage burn-and-gain-2 shot, giving this deck unusually consistent value off its heavy Swamp count, while Creeping Bloodsucker chips away 1 damage and gains life every upkeep. That incidental damage is exactly what Mire Blight and Hooded Assassin need — Mire Blight destroys any enchanted creature that takes damage, and Hooded Assassin can destroy any creature that was damaged that turn, so Dread Presence's burn mode or Conductor of Cacophony's counter-based pings can set up a kill. Nested Ghoul again converts damage taken into 2/2 Zombie tokens, and Feast on the Fallen keeps growing a creature as long as the opponent's life total keeps dropping from all this passive drain.
tips: Dread Presence rewards heavy Swamp count
Use burn mode to enable Mire Blight/Hooded Assassin
Nested Ghoul turns pings into tokens
Feast on the Fallen grows off constant drain
Conductor of Cacophony pings set up kills

decks.description: This is a mono-black/white-splash lifegain vampire deck that turns every point of life gained into damage and value. The core payoff cluster—Marauding Blight-Priest and Epicure of Blood—drains an opponent for 1 life every single time you gain life, while Bloodthirsty Aerialist grows a permanent flying threat off the same trigger. Rodolf Duskbringer ties it all together: its lifelink and deathtouch generate the life gain that both protects it with indestructible and fuels its end step reanimation ability, letting you rebuy creatures from the graveyard for free. Small lifegain sources like Vampire Envoy (gains life on tap) and Creeping Bloodsucker (pings opponents every upkeep while gaining life) exist purely to trigger the payoffs repeatedly, and Oathsworn Vampire can be recast straight from the graveyard whenever you've gained life that turn, giving you resilient recursive value. Removal like Moment of Craving and Feast of Blood double as extra lifegain triggers, and Ill-Gotten Inheritance provides a repeatable damage/lifegain engine that can also be sacrificed for a burst finisher.
tips: Chain small lifegain triggers
Cast Oathsworn Vampire from graveyard after gaining life
Use Rodolf's end step trigger to reanimate
Removal spells double as lifegain
Protect Blight-Priest/Epicure for drain value

decks.description: Similar to the other Fangs builds, this deck centers on triggering lifegain repeatedly to power Marauding Blight-Priest's opponent-draining ability and Bloodthirsty Aerialist's counter growth, while Rodolf Duskbringer's indestructibility and graveyard reanimation ability reward every bit of life you gain. This version leans more into a sacrifice/value subtheme: Vampiric Rites lets you sacrifice a creature for life and a card, which combos nicely with Bloodtracker, whose leaves-the-battlefield trigger draws a card for every +1/+1 counter it accumulated from paying life to grow itself. Falkenrath Noble punishes any creature death (yours or opponent's) with a life-drain trigger, so sacrificing your own creatures to Vampiric Rites also triggers Falkenrath Noble for extra drain. Oathsworn Vampire keeps returning from the graveyard as long as you gained life that turn, and small engines like Creeping Bloodsucker and Vampire Envoy generate the steady trickle of lifegain the whole deck wants. Ill-Gotten Inheritance again offers a repeatable pinger with a late-game sacrifice finisher.
tips: Grow Bloodtracker then sac for cards
Trigger Falkenrath Noble via sacrifice
Recur Oathsworn Vampire after lifegain
Chain lifegain into Blight-Priest drain
Use Vampiric Rites to convert bodies into cards

decks.description: This deck pushes the lifegain-matters theme toward big single-turn payoffs with Tivash, Gloom Summoner, which lets you convert all the life you gained in a turn into a flying Demon token of that size at end of turn—meaning a big lifegain turn (from Vampire Envoy, Creeping Bloodsucker, Moment of Craving, or Certain Death) can produce a genuinely large flier. Bloodbond Vampire and Nirkana Assassin both key off lifegain triggers, growing counters and granting deathtouch respectively, so a well-timed lifegain spell can simultaneously pump your team and make your Assassin a monster-killer. Marauding Blight-Priest keeps punishing the opponent every time you gain life, and Ancient Craving offers a big life-loss-for-cards refill. Oathsworn Vampire's graveyard recursion and Blood Artist's death-trigger drain round out a deck that wants to gain life constantly and turn that into both damage and board presence.
tips: Save big lifegain for Tivash's demon token
Time removal spells to maximize life gained
Deathtouch via Nirkana Assassin after lifegain
Recur Oathsworn Vampire off lifegain
Use Ancient Craving to refuel

decks.description: This build's centerpiece is Vito, Thorn of the Dusk Rose, which converts every instance of lifegain directly into opponent life loss and can grant the whole team lifelink for a turn to snowball drastically. Kalastria Nightwatch turns lifegain into evasion by granting flying, and Bloodbond Vampire grows with every lifegain trigger, so cheap lifegain sources like Vampire Envoy and Creeping Bloodsucker feed multiple payoffs at once. Marauding Blight-Priest doubles up on Vito's drain effect, punishing the opponent further for any life you gain, while Moment of Craving and Feast of Blood are removal spells that also trigger the whole lifegain package. Oathsworn Vampire and Blood Artist provide recursive value and death-trigger drain, and Ill-Gotten Inheritance offers a steady pinger with a late finisher, making this a deck about stacking small lifegain triggers into a wide, evasive vampire board that closes games through drain damage.
tips: Activate Vito's lifelink ability for a swing
Layer Blight-Priest and Vito drain together
Use Kalastria Nightwatch to fly over blockers
Time removal to trigger lifegain payoffs
Recur Oathsworn Vampire off lifegain

cards.title: Vito, Thorn of the Dusk Rose

decks.description: This deck is a classic self-mill zombie tribal build that turns its graveyard into a second hand. Crow of Dark Tidings mills on both entering and dying, feeding Gravecrawler, which can be recast from the graveyard for free as long as you control any Zombie—an easy loop once Lord of the Accursed or other zombies are on board. Gravedigger and Cemetery Recruitment return creatures from the graveyard to hand (with Cemetery Recruitment drawing an extra card if it's a Zombie), while Deadly Plot doubles as removal or a way to reanimate a Zombie straight onto the battlefield. Necromancer's Stockpile turns excess creature cards into card draw and tapped Zombie tokens, and Maalfeld Twins converts its own death into two more 2/2 Zombie bodies, giving the deck resilient go-wide potential. Lord of the Accursed anthem-buffs the team and can grant menace to push through for damage once the board is stabilized.
tips: Recast Gravecrawler from graveyard freely
Mill with Crow of Dark Tidings for value
Use Necromancer's Stockpile to convert extras
Reanimate with Deadly Plot
Lord of the Accursed for menace pushes
